article_title: NASA's Psyche Spacecraft Prepares for May 15 Mars Flyby, Using the Planet's Gravity to Save Propellant on the Way to a Metal Asteroid
summary: Editorially clean. Allowlist warning dismissed — spacedaily.com is a legitimate space-news aggregator and the specific claim attributed to it (satellite-search moonlet-rehearsal) is corroborated in its snapshot.

content_preview.title: NASA's Psyche Spacecraft Prepares for May 15 Mars Flyby, Using the Planet's Gravity to Save Propellant on the Way to a Metal Asteroid
content_preview.summary: Psyche will pass 4,500 kilometers above Mars on May 15 at 12,333 mph, harnessing the planet's gravity to redirect itself toward the metal-rich asteroid Psyche and calibrate its imager, magnetometer, and gamma-ray spectrometer.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

NASA's Psyche spacecraft is on course for a close flyby of Mars on Friday, May 15, 2026, passing 2,800 miles (4,500 kilometers) above the Martian surface at 12,333 mph (19,848 kph), [according to NASA Science](https://science.nasa.gov/blogs/psyche/2026/05/08/nasas-psyche-mission-to-fly-by-mars-for-gravity-assist/). The encounter is a propellant-saving gravity assist designed to bend the probe's trajectory toward its destination, the metal-rich asteroid Psyche, [as NASA's Jet Propuls...

recommendations: Consider adding spacedaily.com to config/source_allowlist.txt as a recognized space-news aggregator.

editor_notes.content_quality: Solid 676-word News piece on the May 15 Mars gravity-assist. Standard Machine Herald structure (Overview, What We Know, The Destination, What We Don't Know). Technical specifics (4,500 km altitude, 12,333 mph velocity, xenon SEP propulsion, Oct 13 2023 Falcon Heavy launch, August 2029 asteroid orbit insertion) handled accurately.
editor_notes.source_verification: Read all 6 snapshots. source-0 NASA Science (12.9k chars): all three direct quotes verbatim — Bairstow 'We are now exactly on target for the flyby...', Elkins-Tanton 'The only reason for this flyby is to get a little help from Mars...', Bell 'The thin crescent on approach and the nearly full Mars view...'. Elkins-Tanton affiliation 'University of California, Berkeley' verbatim; article's 'UC Berkeley' shorthand is correct. Bell at Arizona State University in Tempe verbatim. source-1 JPL release (3.8k chars) duplicates the NASA Science key specifics. source-2 JPL image release (4k chars): May 3 panchromatic snapshot from ~3 million miles, 2-millisecond exposure, broadband filter, seasonal clouds/hazes hypothesis confirmed. source-3 ScienceDaily (13.8k chars): full quote set duplicates NASA Science. source-4 SpaceDaily (8.1k chars): satellite-search moonlet-rehearsal claim confirmed. source-5 JPL Psyche mission press kit (18.2k chars): asteroid Psyche size (~173 mi / 280 km), August 2029 arrival, 26-month orbital phase confirmed.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and properly hedged. The cloud/haze observation is correctly described as a hypothesis rather than a confirmed result.
editor_notes.originality: First Machine Herald coverage of the Psyche Mars flyby preparation.
concerns: Automated 'spacedaily.com not in allowlist' warning: dismissed — SpaceDaily is a recognized space-news outlet and the specific claim attributed to it (moonlet-search rehearsal) is corroborated in the snapshot.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: APPROVE. Tight space-news reporting with all quotes verbatim and all specifics confirmed. No corrections record needed.
